title: Tender Moroccan Chicken Tagine
description: A salty, tender, dairy-free Moroccan chicken tagine with olives and a fragrant spice tadka, perfect for meal‑prep.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a heavy‑bottomed pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the chicken thighs and brown on all sides, about 5 minutes, then remove and set aside.
In the same pot, sauté the sliced onion until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and tomato paste, cooking for 1 minute to release aromas.
Mix in the cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and smoked paprika, letting the spices toast for 30 seconds.
Return the browned chicken to the pot and pour in the vegetable broth, scraping up any browned bits.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat, cover, and simmer for 25 minutes, or until the chicken is tender.
Add the pitted olives and lemon juice, and stir gently; cook for an additional 5 minutes to meld flavors.
Taste and season with salt and pepper if needed, then remove the lid and let the sauce thicken for 2 minutes.
Serve hot over cooked couscous or rice, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
